news_summary: UN-led mediation efforts in the Middle East advance peace initiatives while facilitating crucial fertiliser shipments to support livelihoods.
news_summary_fr: Les efforts de médiation menés par l'ONU au Moyen-Orient font progresser les initiatives de paix tout en facilitant les livraisons essentielles d'engrais destinées à soutenir les moyens de subsistance.
news_summary_es: Las iniciativas de mediación lideradas por la ONU en Oriente Medio impulsan las iniciativas de paz y facilitan al mismo tiempo los envíos de fertilizantes, que son fundamentales para garantizar los medios de subsistencia.

articles.title: Middle East war: UN initiatives support mediation efforts, ‘lifesaving’ fertiliser shipments
articles.summary: Just hours after war broke out in the Middle East last month, United Nations Secretary-General António Guterres warned the Security Council that the fighting risked “igniting a chain of events that no one can control in the most volatile region of the world.”
articles.content:
The humanitarian fallout continues to deepen and is exacerbated by the number of deaths, injuries and damage to civilian infrastructure, and the severe impacts on the global economy are increasing.

While UN efforts to support affected civilians and de-escalate the conflict are ongoing, the Secretary-General has stressed the need to take immediate action to mitigate the consequences.

Wide-ranging impacts

The prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz to maritime trade “is choking the movement of oil, gas, and fertiliser at a critical moment in the global planting season,” he said.

Tanker traffic has dropped by more than 90 per cent, according to the UN Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO), which is threatening agricultural production and global food security.

“The crisis is causing the most significant global humanitarian supply chain disruptions since COVID-19 and the onset of the war in Ukraine,” the UN aid coordination office OCHA added.

“Humanitarian supply lines across the Middle East are being severely disrupted, threatening the timely delivery of lifesaving food, medical items and emergency relief to millions.”

Tweet URL

With the conflict showing no signs of stopping, the Secretary-General this week announced two important initiatives: the appointment of an envoy to spearhead UN peace efforts and the establishment of a dedicated Task Force on the Strait of Hormuz.

Facilitating transit, averting crisis

The Task Force will work to ensure safe, orderly and reliable transit for humanitarian purposes through the critical maritime corridor.

It will be headed by Jorge Moreira da Silva, Executive Director of the United Nations Office for Project Services (UNOPS), which provides infrastructure, procurement and project management services around the world.

Representatives from UN trade body UNCTAD, the International Maritime Organization (IMO), and the International Chamber of Commerce will also be on board.

“Our focus is to facilitate the trade of commercial fertilisers and the movement of related raw materials. We are standing by to support this lifesaving operation,” Mr. Moreira da Silva said in a statement posted on X.

“It is urgent to prevent a massive humanitarian crisis in the Middle East and beyond including in Africa and Asia import-dependent on fertilizers.”

Inspired by previous initiatives

The Task Force draws inspiration from other UN initiatives, including the Verification and Inspection Mechanism in Yemen, the former Black Sea Grain Initiative on exports from Ukraine, and the UN Special Mechanism for Gaza aid delivery outlined in Security Council resolution 2720 (2023).

“The mechanism’s operationalization will be done in close consultation with relevant Member States with full respect for national sovereignty and established international legal frameworks,” said UN Spokesperson Stéphane Dujarric

“If successful, it would also create confidence among Member States on the diplomatic approach to the conflict and constitute a valuable step towards a wider political settlement,” he added.

UN Photo/Eskinder Debebe File photo of Jean Arnault, a former Special Representative of the Secretary-General and Head of the UN Verification Mission in Colombia.

UN envoy appointed

The Secretary-General named veteran French diplomat Jean Arnault as his Personal Envoy to lead UN efforts on the Middle East conflict and its consequences.

He warned that the conflict was “out of control”, reiterating his call on the United States and Israel to stop the war, and for Iran to stop attacking its neighbours.

“It is time to stop climbing the escalation ladder – and start climbing the diplomatic ladder, and return to full respect of international law,” he said.

Mr. Arnault “will be doing everything possible” to support all efforts for mediation and peace, and will be in contact with all parties. He will examine how the conflict is impacting the region and civilians, both there and around the world, as well as consequences for the global economy.

The envoy has nearly 40 years of experience in international diplomacy, especially in the field of peaceful settlements and mediation, and has led UN missions in Africa, Asia, Europe and Latin America. Most recently he served as the Secretary-General’s Personal Envoy on Afghanistan and Regional Issues.
articles.source: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
articles.reasoning: The article reports on significant UN initiatives aimed at mediating the Middle East conflict and ensuring the delivery of lifesaving humanitarian aid, including critical fertiliser shipments, which have broad positive impacts on global food security and civilian well-being. The appointment of an experienced envoy and the creation of a dedicated task force demonstrate concrete, organized efforts to de-escalate conflict and mitigate humanitarian crises, offering hope for peace and stability in a volatile region.
articles.personality_title: UN launches new efforts to ease Middle East crisis and protect food supplies
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – Fighting broke out in the Middle East last month, causing many deaths, injuries, and damage to homes and infrastructure. This conflict also disrupted global trade and worsened food supply problems.

**What happened** – The United Nations responded by appointing Jean Arnault, a skilled diplomat, as a special envoy to help mediate peace talks. They also created a Task Force led by Jorge Moreira da Silva to keep vital shipping routes open, especially for fertilizers needed for farming.

**Impact** – These steps aim to stop the conflict from spreading and to prevent food shortages caused by blocked shipments. Fertilizers are crucial for planting crops, and delays can affect millions of people worldwide, especially in places that rely on imports.

**What's next step** – The Task Force will work with international groups and countries to ensure safe passage for humanitarian goods. Meanwhile, the envoy will talk with all sides to encourage peace talks and reduce violence.

**One-sentence takeaway** – The UN’s new peace and aid efforts focus on easing the Middle East conflict and protecting global food supplies by keeping shipments moving and promoting dialogue.
articles.personality_title_fr: L’ONU lance de nouvelles initiatives pour apaiser la crise au Moyen-Orient et protéger l’approvisionnement alimentaire
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Un conflit a éclaté au Moyen-Orient le mois dernier, causant de nombreux morts, blessés et dégâts aux infrastructures civiles. Cette guerre a aussi perturbé le commerce mondial et aggravé les problèmes d’approvisionnement alimentaire.

**Ce qui s’est passé** – L’ONU a nommé Jean Arnault, un diplomate expérimenté, comme envoyé spécial pour aider à la médiation. Elle a aussi créé une Task Force dirigée par Jorge Moreira da Silva pour garantir la circulation des marchandises essentielles, notamment les engrais nécessaires à l’agriculture.

**Impact** – Ces mesures visent à empêcher l’élargissement du conflit et à éviter les pénuries alimentaires causées par le blocage des expéditions. Les engrais sont indispensables pour les récoltes, et leur retard peut toucher des millions de personnes dans le monde.

**Prochaine étape** – La Task Force travaillera avec des organisations internationales et des pays pour assurer le passage sécurisé des aides humanitaires. L’envoyé spécial discutera avec toutes les parties pour encourager les négociations de paix.

**Résumé en une phrase** – Les nouvelles initiatives de l’ONU cherchent à réduire le conflit au Moyen-Orient et à protéger l’approvisionnement alimentaire mondial en maintenant le commerce et en favorisant le dialogue.
articles.personality_title_es: La ONU lanza nuevas iniciativas para aliviar la crisis en Medio Oriente y proteger el suministro de alimentos
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – El mes pasado estalló un conflicto en Medio Oriente, causando muchas muertes, heridos y daños en infraestructuras civiles. Esta guerra también interrumpió el comercio mundial y empeoró los problemas de suministro de alimentos.

**Qué pasó** – La ONU nombró a Jean Arnault, un diplomático experimentado, como enviado especial para ayudar en la mediación. También creó un Grupo de Trabajo liderado por Jorge Moreira da Silva para mantener abiertas las rutas marítimas vitales, especialmente para los fertilizantes necesarios en la agricultura.

**Impacto** – Estas acciones buscan evitar que el conflicto se extienda y prevenir la escasez de alimentos causada por el bloqueo de envíos. Los fertilizantes son esenciales para plantar cultivos, y los retrasos pueden afectar a millones de personas en todo el mundo.

**Próximo paso** – El Grupo de Trabajo colaborará con organizaciones internacionales y países para asegurar el paso seguro de bienes humanitarios. Mientras tanto, el enviado especial dialogará con todas las partes para fomentar las negociaciones de paz.

**Resumen en una frase** – Los nuevos esfuerzos de la ONU se centran en aliviar el conflicto en Medio Oriente y proteger el suministro mundial de alimentos manteniendo el comercio y promoviendo el diálogo.
articles.image_prompt: A detailed, warm painting of a calm maritime strait with stylized cargo ships carrying large sacks labeled as fertilizer, gently passing through a peaceful channel guarded by abstract, glowing hands symbolizing UN mediation and protection, with soft natural tones of blue, beige, and muted greens emphasizing hope and safe passage.
